subject: maths
alignment_note: MPH 1B numbers to 100 maps directly to Singapore P1 whole numbers; 1A counting to 20 is the base and 1B extends range and representation.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child counts reads writes or represents a number to 100 with materials or symbols.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 number supports modeling numbers to hundreds or beyond; alignment is strong for representation and comparison but evidence must stay concrete.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child builds draws or explains quantity with range and support level recorded.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 explicitly includes tens and ones for numbers up to 100; 1A teen place value grows into 1B two-digit place value.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child decomposes a two-digit number into tens and ones.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 place-value modeling aligns with 1B tens-and-ones representations.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child explains or models tens and ones using base-ten materials drawing or place-value chart.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 includes comparing ordering and number sequences; 1A comparison extends into 1B numbers to 40 and 100.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child compares orders or continues pattern and explains strategy.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP pattern/function supports number patterns and inverse relationships; alignment is moderate because MPH 1B patterns are embedded in number work.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child states the pattern rule or inverse relationship with representation.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 1 number supports combining and partitioning; this 1A objective remains necessary for 1B operations.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child composes decomposes or checks whole and parts.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore framework emphasizes representations and strategy explanation but does not prescribe number bonds as the only model.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child uses a number bond or equivalent model to explain a calculation or relationship.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 includes mental calculation within 20 and addition/subtraction within 100; 1A within-20 strategy remains prerequisite for 1B.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child solves within 20 and shows strategy or check.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 1 number includes joining and taking away plus combining and partitioning; strong fit for early operation evidence.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child uses objects drawings number path or number bonds to explain an addition or subtraction.

subject: maths
alignment_note: MPH 1B addition within 40 and 100 maps directly to Singapore P1 addition/subtraction within 100 and algorithms.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed two-digit addition strategy with representation and support level.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 number includes explaining mental drawn and written strategies for two-digit addition/subtraction.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child subtracts using tens-and-ones drawing number line blocks or written layout and explains result.

subject: maths
alignment_note: China first-stage number and operations supports computation and reasoning across lower-primary ranges; 1B gives concrete assets.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed two-digit calculation with explanation of operation meaning.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 includes algorithms and addition/subtraction within 100; regrouping readiness is acceptable when modeled as tens-and-ones exchange.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed exchange of ten ones for one ten during addition.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 emphasizes strategy explanation more than algorithm labels; strong evidence needs the child to explain exchange meaning.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child trades one ten for ten ones and explains why.

subject: maths
alignment_note: China first-stage quantity relationship and problem solving aligns with representing familiar situations; 1A gives story-problem foundation.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child represents story quantities and explains answer meaning.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore framework emphasizes problem solving reasoning communication and strategies across content domains.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed model operation choice solution and contextual answer.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 includes multiplication concepts and multiplying within 40; MPH 1B equal groups are a direct match.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed equal-group model repeated addition or multiplication expression.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 pattern/function supports patterns and inverse relationships but multiplication as repeated addition appears more strongly in later Phase 3; keep alignment moderate.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child connects equal groups to repeated addition without requiring fact fluency.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 introduces division concepts and dividing within 20; MPH 1B sharing and grouping tasks align directly.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed fair sharing or equal grouping with explanation.

subject: maths
alignment_note: China quantity relationship supports simple life-situation problems; division-specific symbolism is broader than the extracted standard summary.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed grouping or sharing model and explanation of fairness or group count.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 picture graphs directly align with MPH 1B picture graph chapter.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed graph reading comparison or simple question answered with graph evidence.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 data handling supports pictographs and comparing quantities; alignment is strong for interpretation evidence.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child uses category and quantity information from the display.

subject: maths
alignment_note: China first-stage measurement supports hands-on measurement awareness; 1A length assets remain the primary processed layer.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed fair comparison or unit measurement with start point and units recorded.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 uses centimetres while MPH 1A mainly uses non-standard units; alignment is moderate until standard-unit evidence is present.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed length comparison or measurement; note whether units were non-standard or cm.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 includes time to 5 minutes and duration; MPH 1B focuses on hour and half hour so alignment is strong for the covered subset.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child reads matches or draws hour/half-hour time in a routine.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 includes reading time to hour half hour and quarter hour and sequencing time in authentic situations.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed clock and routine connection with prompt level recorded.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 money counting maps directly to MPH 1B money values and amount counting.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed denomination recognition amount counting or value comparison.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 treats money within standard measurement contexts; useful for authentic situations but less specific than Singapore money outcomes.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed money problem with denominations amount and strategy recorded.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore P1 2D shape content aligns with 1A naming sorting and composing shape work; 1B does not deepen this much but lower-primary continuity matters.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed shape identification sorting composition or grid-copy evidence.

subject: maths
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 shape-space is broader than 1A position language; alignment supports spatial vocabulary and directions.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed position description with reference point or simple direction.

subject: maths
alignment_note: Singapore framework explicitly values reasoning communication metacognition and strategies; this objective applies across 1A and 1B.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child explanation using objects drawing equation graph clock money or words.

subject: maths
alignment_note: China first-stage communication and attitude supports sharing ideas responding and persevering with support.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observation of strategy explanation error check question response or representation choice.
